settings.question: What is TPMS and why is my light on?
settings.answer: TPMS stands for Tire Pressure Monitoring System. It's a safety feature (mandatory on all vehicles sold in Canada after 2013) that alerts you when one or more tires are significantly under-inflated. The light can come on for several reasons: a tire losing pressure, cold weather causing pressure to drop, a faulty sensor, a dead sensor battery, or a sensor that wasn't re-programmed after a tire swap. We'll diagnose the exact cause and fix it.

settings.question: How much does it cost to replace a TPMS sensor?
settings.answer: TPMS sensor replacement typically starts at $49 per sensor, which includes the new sensor, programming to your vehicle, and re-mounting and balancing that wheel. Prices vary by vehicle make and model — European luxury vehicles often use more expensive sensors. We'll give you an exact quote before any work begins.

settings.question: Do I need to replace TPMS sensors when getting new tires?
settings.answer: Not necessarily — if your existing sensors are working correctly, they'll transfer to the new tires. However, TPMS sensor batteries typically last 7–10 years, so if your sensors are getting older it can make sense to replace them at the same time as new tires to avoid a return trip. We'll check the age and condition of your sensors and give you an honest recommendation.

settings.question: Can you program aftermarket TPMS sensors?
settings.answer: Yes. We program a wide range of aftermarket (universal) TPMS sensors as well as OEM sensors. Universal sensors are a cost-effective alternative to dealer-priced OEM sensors and work with most vehicle makes and models. We use professional programming tools to ensure the sensors communicate correctly with your vehicle's ECU.

settings.question: How long does TPMS service take?
settings.answer: A TPMS diagnosis typically takes 15–20 minutes. If sensor replacement is needed, add 30–45 minutes per wheel. Most TPMS jobs are completed the same day, often within an hour total. Book online to guarantee your time slot — no waiting around.

settings.heading: TPMS Service Calgary
settings.subheading: Tire pressure warning light on? Prince Tires diagnoses, programs, and replaces TPMS sensors for all makes and models in Calgary — same-day service available.
settings.pricing_intro: Ignoring a TPMS warning can lead to dangerously underinflated tires. Our technicians use professional diagnostic tools to pinpoint faulty sensors, then program and install new ones — all in one visit. We work with OEM and aftermarket sensors for all makes and models.
